message:
feat(core): implement VACUUM INTO and trigger WHEN clause evaluation

Two significant enhancements to the SQL execution layer.

## VACUUM INTO implementation
Adds support for VACUUM INTO <path> which exports the database to a file:

**For file-backed databases:**
- Validates no active transaction (VACUUM cannot run in transaction)
- Uses std::fs::copy() to create atomic copy of database file

**For in-memory databases:**
- Creates new Connection at target path
- Iterates schema copying table definitions
- Clones table data from source to destination

Plain VACUUM (without INTO) remains a no-op pending defragmentation impl.

## Trigger WHEN clause evaluation
Adds trigger_when_matches() helper to evaluate constant WHEN expressions:

```rust
fn trigger_when_matches(when_clause: Option<&Expr>) -> bool {
    let Some(expr) = when_clause else { return true; };
    match eval_join_expr(expr, &[], &[]) {
        Ok(value) => is_sqlite_truthy(&value),
        Err(_) => true,  // Fall back to firing if evaluation fails
    }
}
```

Key behaviors:
- No WHEN clause → trigger always fires
- WHEN 0 / WHEN FALSE → trigger never fires
- WHEN 1 / WHEN TRUE → trigger fires
- WHEN <expr with OLD/NEW> → fires (until pseudo-tables implemented)

Updated fire_triggers_for_insert() and fire_triggers_for_delete() to call
trigger_when_matches() before executing trigger body.

## Tests added
- test_trigger_when_false_constant_does_not_fire: WHEN 0 suppresses trigger
- test_trigger_when_true_constant_fires: WHEN 1 allows trigger to fire

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
